summary: McKenzie reports that his newly registered domain has reached position #4 on Google for 'Bingo Card Creator' after just two weeks, achieved partly through soliciting links from education sites that collect resources for teachers. He distinguishes between ranking for his brand name versus his target commercial keyword ('make bingo cards'), showing early understanding of search intent and keyword value. He recommends Google Sitemaps as a tool for ensuring clean crawling.
key_points: Link building through relevant niche directories (education resource sites) can produce fast SEO results even for brand-new domains
Ranking for your brand name is far less valuable than ranking for the commercial intent keyword people actually search for
Being transparent about commercial intent when soliciting links is both ethical and apparently effective
Google Sitemaps helps ensure error-free crawling, which is foundational for new site SEO
money_quotes: I'm aiming for 100% honesty in the process so I always picked commercial software and made it clear the link was an advertisement.
Apparently that just paid off, because my daily search for Bingo Card Creator puts my home page at #4 on the Internet (!) after that domain has been registered for approximately two weeks (!!)
Now, thats not quite my target search term ("make bingo cards" gets searched far, far more often, as I'm gradually learning from paying for people searching for it)
You want to get crawled without any errors — make it easy for Google to do so as soon as humanly possible.
key_insight: Early SEO wins from branded searches feel exciting but the real value comes from ranking for commercial-intent keywords that actual customers are searching for.
